assertions.text: Output contains four distinct sections: Project Description, Significance, Creative Approach, and Impact/Distribution
The funder's stated priorities are addressed explicitly in the narrative
Output ends with a Next Step note identifying weakest section and what to prepare before submitting

four-sections.note: All four sections present with appropriate word counts

funder-priorities.note: Social impact, underrepresented voices, and international relevance explicitly addressed

next-step.note: Next Step identifies Impact section as weakest (no distribution evidence yet) and flags budget narrative requirement

four-sections.note: Four sections present; Creative Approach section thinner due to limited input

funder-priorities.note: Arts council priorities addressed only generically — user did not specify funder criteria, so the skill defaults to general cultural value language

next-step.note: Next Step correctly identifies need to check funder-specific word count limits

four-sections.note: All four sections present; Nordic context well-handled

funder-priorities.note: Nordisk Film & TV Fond priorities (Nordic stories, co-production, cultural preservation) explicitly named and addressed

next-step.note: Next Step identifies need for co-funding letter from Swedish partner

summary.notes: 8 of 9 passed. One failure on minimal input: without specific funder criteria, the skill produces generic significance language. This is a known limitation documented in the skill.
